Quick Answers

Common questions before you call

How do I get a quote?
Text or email photos of the job. We send back a price the same day — no site visit needed in most cases.
Is haul-off included in demo jobs?
Yes. Demolition quotes typically include haul-off. We confirm before starting so there are no surprises.
Can you do same-day jobs?
Sometimes, yes — depending on schedule. Call us directly to check availability.
Are you insured for commercial work?
Yes. We're fully insured and TX HUB certified, making us eligible for government and commercial contracts.

Send us pictures.
We'll give you a straight answer.

Why Clients Call Us

Common pain points we solve

Instructions that make no sense
We've built hundreds of these. We know the quirks, the shortcuts, and where manufacturers get it wrong.
Missing or wrong hardware
We ID missing parts upfront. If a supply run is needed, we handle it — we'll let you know before we go.
Safety concerns for kids
We anchor playsets and trampolines when needed and double-check structural integrity before we leave.
Lost weekends
Text us the model number. We show up, build it, clean up, and you're done. Weekends reclaimed.
No tools or muscle
No tools required from you. We bring everything.
Service Details

What to expect on every job

Playset Assembly

Full assembly including hardware management, anchoring to ground or concrete, and site cleanup. We've done dozens in Houston and San Antonio.

Trampoline Assembly

Frame, springs, mat, safety pad, and enclosure net — built solid. All brands and sizes.

Gazebo Assembly

Panels, roof, and full anchoring to deck, pavers, or ground. We work through Texas heat so you don't have to.

Pergola Assembly

Freestanding or wall-attached. We handle the heavy lifting, leveling, and anchoring.

Fitness Equipment

Treadmills, cable machines, squat racks, rowing machines, ellipticals — fully assembled and tested.

TV Mounting

Stud mount, drywall anchor, or concrete — all wall types. Clean cable management included.

FAQs

Assembly questions answered

How do I get an assembly quote?
Text us the brand and model number along with a photo of the product box. We'll give you a straight price — most quotes come back same day.
What if parts are missing from my kit?
We identify missing items before we start. If a supply run is needed, we'll let you know and handle it — additional material cost applies.
Do you anchor playsets and gazebos?
Yes. Ground anchoring is recommended for most outdoor structures and is typically included or quoted as a add-on depending on surface type.
How long does assembly take?
A trampoline takes 1.5–3 hours. A large playset or gazebo is typically 4–8 hours. We give you a realistic timeline with your quote.
Do you assemble items you didn't sell?
Yes. We assemble any brand or item — just share the model or send the box photo.
Do you serve my area?
We cover San Antonio, Houston, and surrounding counties. Text your zip code and we'll confirm.
Skip the instructions.
We've got it.

Text us the model number. Free quote back same day.

Get Your Free Quote → 📞 210-827-2125

What We Demo

Full Demolition Service List

Why Clients Call Us

Common pain points we solve

Dangerous DIY demo
Demo has risks most people underestimate — structural collapse, asbestos risk, buried utilities. We handle it safely.
Disposal headaches
Haul-off is typically included. We handle the debris, the dump runs, and the disposal — you don't lift a finger.
Mess and damage risk
We protect surrounding structures and surfaces. We work clean and leave clean.
Unclear pricing
We quote by scope. Send photos and get a straight price — no hidden fees.
Tight project timelines
We coordinate with contractors and GCs. We can often mobilize quickly when you're on a deadline.
Notable Projects

We've handled the big ones.

4,200 sq ft Office Demo

Full commercial interior demolition of a 4,200 sq ft office building in San Antonio. Coordinated with Waterman Construction (~$18,000 project).

Science Lab Tearout

Interior demo of a science lab facility at Texas Biomedical Research Institute in San Antonio (~$13,000 project).

Tower of the Americas

Commercial kitchen demo and infrastructure relocation at Tower of the Americas in San Antonio (~$7,200 project).

FAQs

Demolition questions answered

Is haul-off included?
Debris removal is typically included in our demo quotes. We confirm the full scope before starting so there are no surprises.
Do I need a permit for shed or deck demo?
Most small residential demolition jobs in Houston and San Antonio don't require permits — but we recommend confirming with your municipality for anything structural or in a HOA.
How long does shed demolition take?
Most residential sheds take 2–4 hours for demo and load-out. Larger or more complex structures will take longer — we'll estimate with your quote.
Can you work around my contractor's schedule?
Yes. We coordinate with GCs and property managers regularly. Let us know your timeline and we'll make it work.
Do you handle mobile home demolition?
Yes — we've done it. Send photos for a quote.
Ready to take it down?

Send photos for a free quote. Most quotes back same day.

Get Your Free Quote → 📞 210-827-2125

Why Clients Call Us

Common pain points we solve

The pile you've been ignoring
No judgment. We've seen it all. One call and it's gone.
Move-out deadlines
Text us. If the schedule allows, we can often get there same day or next day.
Don't know where stuff goes
We handle proper disposal. You don't have to figure out the dump or recycling.
Heavy and awkward items
Appliances, concrete, mattresses — we handle the lifting so you don't have to.
Contractor debris after a project
Post-construction cleanouts, dumpster alternatives, and debris haul-off for your job site.
FAQs

Junk removal questions

How do I get a junk removal quote?
Send a photo of the pile or area. We price by volume and difficulty. Most quotes are same day.
What do you take?
Most non-hazardous items: furniture, appliances, yard debris, construction waste, concrete, metal, old fencing, and general junk.
What won't you take?
Hazardous materials, chemicals, paint, oil, batteries, and certain electronics. We'll let you know if something doesn't qualify.
Can you sort or donate items before hauling?
Yes — flag items for donation before we start and we'll set them aside.
Do you take concrete and heavy debris?
Yes. Send photos and we'll quote it.
Let's get rid of it.

Text us a photo. Free quote back same day.

Why Clients Call Us

Common pain points

Old fence falling apart
We remove the old fence and haul it off, then build the new one. One company, one call.
Storm damage
Quick turnaround for fence repair and replacement after storms.
Untreated wood degrading
Staining and sealing dramatically extends fence life in the Texas climate. We do both.
No one to remove the old fence
We handle demo and disposal before the new install.
FAQs

Fencing questions answered

How do I get a fence quote?
Send us photos of your yard/property line and let us know the approximate length and fence type you want. Most quotes are same day.
Do you remove the old fence before installing?
Yes. We demo and haul off the old fence as part of the installation process.
What type of wood do you use?
Typically cedar or pressure-treated pine depending on preference and budget. We'll walk you through the options.
How soon after installation should I stain?
New cedar can be stained within a few weeks. Pressure-treated wood typically needs 6 months to dry before staining. We'll advise based on your material.
Ready for a new fence?

Send photos and property dimensions for a free quote.

Get Your Free Quote → 📞 210-827-2125

FAQs

Commercial questions answered

Are you SAM registered?
Yes. We are registered in the System for Award Management (SAM), making us eligible for federal contracting work.
What is TX HUB certification?
TX HUB (Historically Underutilized Business) certification qualifies us for state and local government contracting programs in Texas.
Can you work with our general contractor?
Yes. We coordinate with GCs regularly, follow site protocols, and communicate on your schedule.
Do you handle large-scale commercial demolition?
Yes — up to full interior tearouts of multi-thousand sq ft commercial spaces. Send your scope and we'll quote it.
How do commercial clients get in contact?
Call or text the owner directly at {PHONE}, or email {EMAIL}. No dispatch, no delay.
Send us your scope.

Call or email directly. No dispatch, no middleman.

About Two Guys One Job LLC

Two guys. One job.
Zero worries.

Owner-operated since 2021. Assembly, demolition, junk removal, fencing, and site clearing across Houston and San Antonio. We treat your project like our own.

Our Story

Built on showing up and doing the work right.

Two Guys One Job LLC was founded in 2021 in San Antonio, TX with a simple idea: give homeowners and businesses a reliable, owner-operated option for the jobs that need to get done — without the runaround.

When you call us, you're talking to Daniel — the owner, the estimator, and often one of the guys doing the work. That means faster answers, honest pricing, and someone who actually cares about the outcome.

Nearly four years in, we've handled everything from Christmas morning playset builds in freezing temperatures to 4,200 sq ft commercial interior demolitions. We've worked with homeowners, general contractors, property managers, and government-adjacent facilities. Every job gets the same standard: show up, do it right, clean up, done.

"2 Guys, 1 Job, 0 Worries."
Our mission statement — and our standard.

FAQ

Straight answers.
No runaround.

Everything you want to know before you call — answered plainly.

Getting a Quote

How do I get a quote?
Text or email photos of the job with a brief description. We respond with a price the same day in most cases. No site visit required for most jobs.
Are estimates really free?
Yes. Always. No trip charge, no obligation.
How quickly can I get scheduled?
Often within the week. Same-day is possible depending on our schedule — call to check availability.
Do you serve my area?
We cover San Antonio/Bexar County and Houston/Harris County, plus surrounding areas. Text your zip code to confirm.

Assembly

What do you assemble?
Playsets, trampolines, gazebos, pergolas, furniture, fitness equipment, TV mounting, outdoor playhouses, basketball goals, and more.
What if parts are missing?
We identify missing items before starting. If a supply run is needed, we'll confirm with you — additional material cost applies.
Do you anchor structures?
Yes. Ground anchoring is recommended for most outdoor structures and is included or quoted as a line item.
How long does assembly take?
A trampoline: 1.5–3 hours. Large playset or gazebo: 4–8 hours. We estimate per job.

Demolition

Is haul-off included?
Debris removal is typically included in demo quotes. We confirm the full scope before starting so there are no surprises.
Do I need a permit for residential demo?
Most small residential demo jobs (sheds, decks) don't need permits in Houston or San Antonio — but check with your municipality for structural work or HOA rules.
How long does shed demolition take?
2–4 hours for most residential sheds. Larger structures take longer — we estimate with your quote.
Do you do selective demolition?
Yes. We can remove specific walls, sections, or structures while leaving others intact.

Junk & Fencing

What junk do you take?
Most non-hazardous items: furniture, appliances, yard waste, construction debris, concrete, metal, and general clutter.
What won't you take?
Hazardous materials, chemicals, paint, batteries, and certain electronics.
Do you remove old fences before installing a new one?
Yes — we demo the old fence and haul it off as part of the install.

Commercial & General

Are you insured?
Yes. Fully insured for residential and commercial work.
Are you TX HUB and SAM certified?
Yes. TX HUB certified (Historically Underutilized Business) and SAM registered for federal contracting.
How do I reach the owner directly?
Call or text 210-827-2125 or email dsantallana@twoguysonejob.org. You're talking to Daniel — no dispatch, no middleman.
Can you handle same-day jobs?
Sometimes — depending on schedule. Call to check. We'll always be upfront about availability.
Still have a question?

Call or text us directly. We'll give you a straight answer.
210-827-2125
